skill.name: zimage-skill
skill.description: Generate AI images from text prompts through the ModelScope Z-Image-Turbo API.
skill.summary: Creates images from natural language prompts using ModelScope Z-Image-Turbo.
skill.author: yizhiyanhua-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified real network, environment variable, and filesystem behavior in generate.py. The API key is sent to the documented ModelScope endpoint and the README command detections are markdown examples, so no confirmed malicious intent or prompt injection was found. Publish with a warning because the skill uses a third-party API, handles an API token, and writes image files to caller-provided paths.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party API Receives Prompt Data and API Token
medium_findings.description: The generator reads MODELSCOPE_API_KEY and sends it as a bearer token to the ModelScope image generation API with the user prompt. This is expected for the skill, but it exposes prompts and credentials to a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly constructs an Authorization header from MODELSCOPE_API_KEY and posts prompt data to the configured ModelScope URL. The endpoint matches the documented service, so this is a real privacy and credential-handling risk, not evidence of malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Caller-Controlled Image Output Path
medium_findings.description: The script accepts an optional output path from command-line arguments and saves the downloaded image there. This is normal functionality, but users or agents could overwrite files if they provide sensitive paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The output path is explicitly taken from sys.argv and passed to Pillow save(). There is no evidence of hidden file targeting in code, but arbitrary writes are possible by design.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Triggered External Command Heuristics
low_findings.description: Most external command detections are fenced README or SKILL.md examples showing installation and usage commands. They are documentation text, not executable code paths inside the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are inside markdown code fences or prose examples. No subprocess, shell execution, eval, or dynamic command execution appears in generate.py.

low_findings.title: Home Directory References Are Installation Guidance
low_findings.description: The hidden home-directory findings point to documentation references for Claude skill installation and settings. They do not show code that secretly reads or modifies hidden files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are instructions or example paths. The runtime script writes only the requested image output path and does not access ~/.claude itself.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Entropy Heuristics Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The cited README and SKILL text describes image generation and does not contain cryptographic code or encoded payloads. No evidence found of obfuscation or weak cryptographic implementation in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found ordinary documentation text at the cited locations and a short Python script with no cryptographic primitives. The high-entropy README_CN.md signal is plausibly caused by Chinese text, not packed content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network, Credential, and File Write Combination
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines a third-party network API, environment credential access, and local image file writes. This combination is legitimate for image generation but should remain visible to users before installation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The combination is directly present in the runtime script. The same review found no unrelated endpoint, hidden prompt injection, or covert credential collection.

content.value_statement: Teams often need quick visual drafts without leaving their coding assistant. This skill turns text prompts into image files through the ModelScope Z-Image-Turbo API.
actual_capabilities: Generates images from natural language prompts using Tongyi-MAI/Z-Image-Turbo.
Calls the ModelScope asynchronous image generation API.
Reads MODELSCOPE_API_KEY from the local environment.
Accepts a prompt and optional output path from the command line.
Polls task status for up to 120 seconds before timing out.
Downloads the generated image and saves it as a local file.
limitations: Requires a valid ModelScope API key and network access.
Sends prompts and the API token to a third-party service.
Provides no local image editing or post-processing controls.
Can overwrite files if a conflicting output path is supplied.

use_cases.title: Draft Blog Illustrations
use_cases.description: Create quick concept images for articles, tutorials, and documentation drafts.

use_cases.title: Create Product Mockup Art
use_cases.description: Generate background images, hero concepts, and visual directions for early product work.

use_cases.title: Produce Social Visual Ideas
use_cases.description: Turn campaign ideas into draft visuals for review before final design work.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a single image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a calm mountain lake at sunrise and save it as lake.jpg.

prompt_templates.title: Styled Illustration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a watercolor illustration of a small robot reading in a library, warm light, soft colors.

prompt_templates.title: Marketing Concept
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate a draft asset for review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a clean product launch background with abstract technology shapes, bright natural light, and space for headline text.

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Art Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt with composition and output naming.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a cinematic city street scene after rain, reflective pavement, realistic lighting, wide composition, no text, save as campaign_concept.png.

output_examples.input: Generate a cute cartoon avatar with blue hair and a friendly expression.
output_examples.output: A local avatar image file is created with a cartoon character matching the requested description.

output_examples.input: Create a tech-themed article illustration about artificial intelligence.
output_examples.output: A draft technology illustration is saved locally for review and possible article use.

output_examples.input: Draw a peaceful Chinese ink landscape with mountains, mist, and a small boat.
output_examples.output: A landscape image is generated using the requested traditional visual style.

best_practices: Use a dedicated ModelScope API key with limited exposure.
Save outputs to a project-specific folder to avoid overwriting important files.
Include subject, style, composition, and quality details in prompts.
anti_patterns: Do not include secrets, private data, or unreleased product details in prompts.
Do not save generated files over source code or configuration files.
Do not assume generated images are licensed for every commercial use without review.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires MODELSCOPE_API_KEY in the local environment before generation can run.

faq.question: Where are images saved?
faq.answer: Images are saved to the requested output path, or to result_image.jpg when no path is provided.

faq.question: Does it work offline?
faq.answer: No. It calls the ModelScope API and downloads the generated image from the returned URL.

faq.question: Can it use Chinese prompts?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation states that Chinese and English prompts are supported.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It can be published with a warning for third-party API use, credential handling, and local file writes.

faq.question: Does it edit existing images?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed script generates new images from text prompts only.
